prompt:
Develop a ChargeProcure Field Operations system for managing offline EV charging site operations, procurement, and compliance documentation on iPhone and iPad. The app serves three roles: Administrator (defines permissions, pricing rules, and audit reviews), Site Technician (executes charger commands, logs receipts/returns, and drafts field notes), and Finance Approver (handles invoices, reconciliation, and write-offs). Users authenticate with a locally stored username and password (minimum 10 characters, at least one number) and can optionally unlock with FaceID/TouchID via LocalAuthentication; after 5 failed attempts, the app enforces a 15‑minute lockout. The home experience is a dashboard optimized for one-hand iPhone use and iPad Split View, with Dark Mode support, Dynamic Type scaling, Safe Area compliance, and haptic feedback for critical actions (e.g., remote stop confirmation). Content creation and management is used for operational bulletins and site notes: technicians can create/edit drafts with autosave every 10 seconds, choose Markdown or WYSIWYG, set a 280‑character summary, attach a cover image, schedule publish/unpublish by local device time, pin high-priority bulletins, and assign a 0–100 recommendation weight; every publish creates a read-only version in history with restore. The procurement closed loop supports requisition → RFQ price comparison → PO → receipt/return → invoice → reconciliation → payment, including partial receiving and partial invoicing, price/tax variance handling (flag variances over $25.00 or over 2.0%), statement generation by vendor and month, and exception write-offs capped at $250.00 per invoice with mandatory note and approver sign-off. Pricing and rules cover charger-related services (e.g., one-way relocation, add-on adapters) with tiered pricing by vehicle class/store/time window/rental duration, deposits and pre-authorization rules tracked locally (status only, no online payment), and rule versioning with effective start/end timestamps. Real-time status telemetry and control is supported through locally connected charger interfaces (vendor SDKs stored on-device), tracking online/fault/idle/charging, with remote start/stop, soft reset, parameter push, and command acknowledgments that must confirm within 8 seconds or be marked "Pending Review." Multi-role access control enforces resource-level permissions, logs operation audits (who/what/when/device), and records permission-change history.

The app is built in Objective‑C using UIKit with Auto Layout and Core Data for fully on-device persistence; no server backend, remote database, or third-party network services are permitted. Core data models include User, Role, Permission, AuditEvent, Charger, ChargerEvent, Command, ProcurementCase (Requisition/RFQ/PO/Receipt/Return/Invoice/Payment sub-entities), Vendor, PricingRule (versioned), CouponPackage, Bulletin, BulletinVersion, and ReportExport, with immutable audit/event entities to preserve traceability. Attachments such as cover images and invoices are stored in the app sandbox as files referenced by Core Data; the system enforces a 25 MB per-attachment limit, validates file type by magic header (PDF/JPEG/PNG), and runs a weekly cleanup job that deletes unreferenced files and drafts older than 90 days unless pinned. Performance targets include a cold start under 1.5 seconds on supported devices (minimum iOS 15.0, iPhone and iPad), pagination for long histories, and explicit memory warning handling (release image caches, pause analytics rendering). Data statistics and visual analytics are computed locally from stored events and procurements: consecutive-day streaks, completion rates per workflow stage, distribution heatmaps by hour/day, comparisons by task/group/time, trend analysis over 7/30/90 days, and anomaly flags for gaps over 72 hours or volatility exceeding 3× a 30-day moving average; exports generate CSV/PDF files to local storage and use iOS share sheet for offline transfer. Background task management is used to queue command retries, compute overnight reports while on power, and apply battery optimization by deferring non-urgent analytics and large exports when Low Power Mode is enabled.
